## Evan Carter: April 2022 Investment Summary

**At Purchase (April 20, 2022):** Carter was healthy after missing most of 2021 with a small fracture in his spine, and was just beginning his 2022 season as the Rangers' No. 9 prospect. He showed the same standout potential he had hinted at in 2021, with a mix of speed, defense, hittability and power projection that made him one of the Rangers' most attractive prospects. However, he was largely unknown to the broader market—a draft day surprise from 2020.

**Why It Succeeded:** Carter's 2022 season was exceptional, batting .295/.397/.489 with career highs in home runs (12), triples (10), doubles (21), RBI (73), runs (86) and stolen bases (28) over 106 games between Hickory (A+) and Frisco (AA), earning the Tom Grieve Player of the Year award. More crucially, Carter was ranked as the 26th overall prospect in baseball by Baseball America, the 41st overall prospect by MLB Pipeline, and the 53rd overall prospect by The Athletic's Keith Law during the 2023 off-season, and in 23 games in 2023, he batted .306/.413/.645
